title: senecio chilensis var. ceratophyllus
intro: Senecio chilensis var. ceratophyllus, commonly known as Kaki-toki or Chilean fireweed, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the high-altitude regions of the Andes Mountains. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid content, particularly senecionine. The health benefits of Senecio chilensis var. ceratophyllus include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, tincture, or infused oil to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although caution should be exercised due to its potential toxicity.
